segments.text: Thursday of last week, the Biden administration released a proposal that would forbid schools

segments.text: and colleges across the United States from enacting outright bans on transgender athletes
segments.speaker: Chad Kultgen

segments.text: participation in sports.
segments.speaker: Chad Kultgen

segments.text: If finalized, the proposal would become enshrined as a provision of Title IX, the landmark gender
segments.speaker: Chad Kultgen

segments.text: queer legislation enacted in 1972.
segments.speaker: Chad Kultgen

segments.text: While the proposal is meant to be a step forward for trans athletes and trans rights in general,
segments.speaker: Chad Kultgen

segments.text: the language of the proposal is vague enough to allow for interpretation that could lead
segments.speaker: Chad Kultgen

segments.text: to discriminatory policies being developed on a school-by-school basis.
segments.speaker: Chad Kultgen

segments.text: 16 states currently have bans in effect covering high school, interscholastic sports.
segments.speaker: Chad Kultgen

segments.text: Some also extend to intramural, club, or college sports.
segments.speaker: Chad Kultgen

segments.text: Under the proposal, no school or college that receives federal funding would be allowed
segments.speaker: Chad Kultgen

segments.text: to impose a one-size-fits-all policy that categorically bans trans students from playing
segments.speaker: Chad Kultgen

segments.text: sports on teams consistent with their gender identity.
